Jerusalem was so strong that its people believed an enemy could never enter it. But sin caused God to withdraw his protection, and then it was as weak as any other city. Zedekiah had his eyes put out; so he who had shut his eyes against the clear light of God's word was condemned to darkness. Whoever does not believe God’s words will be convinced by events. Observe the wonderful changes in God’s care, how uncertain earthly possessions are; and see the just dealings of God: but whether the Lord makes people poor or rich, nothing will profit them while they remain devoted to their sins.

The servants of God alone are prepared for all events; and they are delivered and comforted, while the wicked suffer. They often receive more kindness from those who do not believe, than from hypocritical people who claim to be religious. The Lord will raise up friends for them, do them good, and fulfill all his promises.

Here is a message to assure Ebed-melech of a reward for his great kindness to Jeremiah. Because you have put your trust in me, the Lord says. God rewards people’s actions according to their character. Those who trust God in the way of duty, as this good man did, will find that their hope does not fail even in times of greatest danger.
